Turkey.status: ELIMINATED — no mathematical path to advancement.

United States.status: Already qualified, guaranteed top spot regardless of result today. Possible squad rotation.

TUR.CRITICAL: Turkey dominates possession (71.6%, 78.5%) and shot volume (30, 32 shots) in BOTH matches yet has lost both 0-2 and 0-1 — elite underlying numbers, terrible conversion. This is NOT a defensive-underdog team; it's an attacking team that can't finish. Offsides (3, 2) and corners (8, 12) are genuine attacking-volume signals, not noise.

context_flags: TUR_ELIMINATED_BUT_DANGEROUS: Turkey has 0 points but dominates possession and shot volume in every match — pride/dead-rubber framing does NOT mean low output.
USA_ALREADY_QUALIFIED: USA may rotate personnel since group position is locked. Slight downgrade to USA attacking intensity possible, no hard data on lineup yet.

TUR_OFFSIDE_LESSON: In the most recent TUR-PAR match we wrongly applied the defensive-underdog 2+ offsides NO-family to Turkey and lost -20.26 RBP — Turkey is an ATTACKING team that racks up offsides from sheer volume of forward play, win or lose. Do NOT shade this down today.

TUR_CORNERS_VS_MARKET_GAP: ESPN average corners for Turkey is 10.0/game (8, 12) but the market prices Over4.5 at only 47.85%. Likely explanation: Turkey's first two opponents (AUS, PAR) sat in very deep low-possession blocks (28%, 21.5%), funneling play wide and generating corners; USA holds the ball themselves (61-65% possession), which should reduce Turkey's territorial share and corner count relative to the first two matches.

USA_more_fouls_signal: USA fouls 13,12 (tight, consistent) vs TUR fouls 4,14 (highly variable, driven by possession share — TUR fouls less when they have the ball). Turkey will likely have more possession again today even against USA, supporting USA fouling more.

questions.text: Will United States commit more fouls than Türkiye?
questions.reasoning: USA fouls consistently 12-13/game. Turkey fouls inversely track their possession share (4 fouls at 71.6% possession vs AUS; 14 fouls at 78.5% possession vs PAR — so possession share alone doesn't fully explain it, but TUR's foul count is erratic vs USA's stable ~12.5). Turkey likely holds more of the ball again today even against USA, supporting USA fouling more. Normal-approximation comparison gives ~0.70 raw; shaded down for Turkey's small-sample volatility.

questions.text: Will Türkiye be caught offside 2 or more times?
questions.reasoning: TUR offsides: 3 (vs AUS), 2 (vs PAR). λ=2.5 → Poisson P(≥2)=0.71. Direct correction from the TUR-PAR postmortem: we previously misapplied the defensive-underdog NO-family to Turkey and lost badly (-20.26 RBP) because Turkey is actually a high-possession, high-volume attacking team regardless of scoreline or elimination status. Today we trust the raw rate, shaded only slightly down for USA being a tougher defensive opponent than AUS/PAR.

questions.text: Will the second half have more total goals than the first half?
questions.reasoning: Direct market: Highest-scoring-half, Second_Half = 46.01%. Trust the liquid market.

questions.text: Will Türkiye score the first goal of the game AND United States score in the second half?
questions.reasoning: Compound: P(TUR scores first)=0.393, P(USA scores in 2H)=0.636. Naive independence product = 0.250. If Turkey scores first, USA likely needs to chase, slightly raising their 2H scoring odds (positive correlation) — small upward bump applied, consistent with the same compound-question adjustment used in USA-PAR.

questions.text: Will a penalty kick be awarded OR a red card be shown in the match?
questions.reasoning: Direct compound market: Penalty Yes=28.48%, Red/sent-off Yes=11.01%. P(OR) = 1-(0.7152×0.8899) = 0.3635. This question family has been a reliable +EV source all tournament.
Smarkets FTR: Turkey 25.32%. Direct tier-1 market.

questions.text: Will the match have 3 or more total goals?
questions.reasoning: Direct market: Over2.5 = 60.62%. Both teams have shown attacking output (TUR shot volume, USA conversion) — consistent with an over-leaning market.

questions.text: Will Türkiye have 5 or more corner kicks?
questions.reasoning: ESPN average is a huge 10.0 corners/game (8, 12) — but those came against AUS/PAR who sat extremely deep (28%, 21.5% possession). USA holds the ball themselves (61-65% possession), which should compress Turkey's territorial dominance relative to the first two matches. Market prices Over4.5 at 47.85%. Blend: weight Turkey's genuine attacking-volume skill against the reduced territorial gap vs a possession-based opponent.

questions.text: Will Orkun Kökçü score or assist a goal (excluding own goals)?
questions.reasoning: Smarkets offer-only (illiquid): 0.2778, adjusted ×0.945 = 0.262. Kökçü is Turkey's primary creative outlet on a team generating huge shot volume (30, 32 shots) — some directional support for involvement, but illiquid market and 0 points in 2 matches caps confidence.

questions.text: Will Folarin Balogun score a goal (excluding own goals)?
questions.reasoning: Anytime goalscorer offer-only: 0.4274, adjusted ×0.945 = 0.404. Balogun is USA's lead striker; USA has scored in every match (4, 2 goals). Caveat: Balogun had 0 SOT in the AUS game despite a liquid 0.73 SOT market — known variance risk on this specific player's props.

strongest_picks: Q2: Turkey 2+ offsides = 0.65 (HIGHEST CONFIDENCE — direct lesson from TUR-PAR loss; Turkey is an attacking-volume team, not a defensive underdog, regardless of elimination status)
Q6: Turkey wins = 0.25 (HIGH CONFIDENCE — direct market)
Q5: Penalty or red card = 0.36 (HIGH CONFIDENCE — reliable compound market family all tournament)
Q7: 3+ total goals = 0.61 (HIGH CONFIDENCE — direct market)

Q1_USA_more_fouls.note: USA did NOT foul more than Turkey despite the possession-inverse pattern from GD1/GD2. We over-trusted the foul-vs-possession heuristic; Turkey's foul count surged in this match (likely chasing/defending a lead at different points in a back-and-forth 3-2 game).

Q2_TUR_2plus_offsides.note: Direct vindication of the TUR-PAR lesson applied here — Turkey is a high-possession, high-volume attacking team and racked up offsides again. Biggest single win of the match.

Q6_TUR_wins.note: Turkey won 3-2 — a genuine upset relative to both our 25% and crowd's 31%. Direct market (Smarkets FTR 25.3%) was wrong; high-shot-volume, low-conversion teams can break through given enough chances.

Q8_TUR_5plus_corners.note: Essentially a push — the possession-compression theory (USA holds the ball more than AUS/PAR did) was directionally correct; Turkey's corner volume came down from their 8/12 average.

Q10_Balogun_scores.note: Second straight match Balogun's prop missed (0 SOT vs AUS in GD2, no goal here). Pattern building: illiquid/moderate Balogun goal/SOT props have now failed 2/2 — treat his attacking props with more skepticism going forward.

key_confirmations: Q2 Turkey 2+ offsides CONFIRMED the corrected read from TUR-PAR — Turkey's attacking-volume offside rate is a real, repeatable signal, not noise from one match.

Q6 Turkey win at 25% was the single biggest miss — a true upset (3-2) that no market or model signal flagged. Worth flagging as tournament-stage variance: eliminated teams with elite underlying shot volume (30, 32 shots/game) can convert when it doesn't matter to the bigger picture.
